description_long: This humanoid robot stands upright on two mechanical legs, each featuring multiple articulated joints and robust feet designed for stability. Its torso is broad and armored, with a prominent chest plate and two large cylindrical structures extending from the back, possibly functioning as power units or exhausts. The arms are asymmetrical: the left arm ends in a claw-like manipulator, while the right arm is bulkier and appears to incorporate a tool or weapon. The head is integrated into the torso, with a smooth, helmet-like dome and no visible facial features. Tubes and cables run from the back to the arms and torso, suggesting a complex internal power or hydraulic system. The robot is mounted on a circular, dark wooden platform, emphasizing its display or operational readiness.
description: A robust, armored humanoid robot with articulated limbs, a dome-shaped head, and prominent back-mounted cylinders, standing on a circular wooden platform.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad chest plate, dome-shaped head, asymmetrical arms, and detailed leg joints.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the claw-like left hand, back-mounted cylinders, and visible tubing running from the back to the arm.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the full structure of the back-mounted cylinders, cable connections, and the mechanical design of the legs and feet.
description_view_3: Right side view emphasizes the bulkier right arm, additional tubing, and the mechanical articulation of the legs.
attribute_pose: Standing upright with both legs straight, left arm extended forward with a claw open, right arm bent at the elbow and held slightly forward, head facing straight ahead, and torso upright.
attribute_clothing_style: No clothing; exterior consists of integrated metal and plastic panels with visible mechanical and hydraulic components.
attribute_facial_features: No visible facial features; head is a smooth, dome-shaped structure with no eyes, mouth, or nose.
attribute_limb_articulation: Arms and legs have multiple hinge and rotary joints at the shoulders, elbows, wrists, hips, knees, and ankles, allowing for a wide range of motion; hands and feet are mechanical and articulated.
